title: Logo size on forms
excerpt: When you update your logo on forms it is so tiny! I’d love for them to fix the feature to allow the size to be like a normal size. It’s literally microscopic.

posts.author: AshleyB (Ashle Bechtloff)
posts.cookedHtml: When you update your logo on forms it is so tiny! I’d love for them to fix the feature to allow the size to be like a normal size. It’s literally microscopic.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @AshleyB

There is a workaround for this - using the form description that supports HTML as the Logo, Title, and Description.

For example, if you paste this block of HTML in your form description, it will give you a fully customisable Logo, Title, and description:

<div style="text-align: center; width: 100%;">
  <img src="https://files.monday.com/use1/logos/5459733/original/eazyapps_logo_102790.png" alt="Contact Us" style="width: 100px; height: auto; margin-bottom: 10px;">
  <label style="font-size: 24px; display: block; margin-bottom: 5px;">Contact Us</label>
  <label style="font-size: 16px; color: #666; display: block;">We'd love to hear from you! Please fill out the form below.</label>
</div>

If you have any issues adjusting the code to your needs, you can paste it in ChatGPT and give it instructions.

monday does limit the space allocated to the description box, so if you find that monday forms do not give you the styling flexibility you are looking for, please try out our monday app form builder EasyForm.

EasyForm supports HTML in the form description and in the description of each question in the form.

You can install it form the monday marketplace here.

Check out our documentation site here.

Book a demo or support call with us here, or contact us by mail at easyappssupport@easyappshq.com.

posts.author: Sam_H (SammH)
posts.cookedHtml: where are you getting that img src from? when i try to open my logo uploaded to the file column in a new tab to get the image source, it juts redownloads it. i can’t seem to find the files.(company).monday.com source that you have referenced here (and for some reason it wont take an img src from another public option)

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Okay, here’s the corrected version with a few minor tweaks for clarity:

Hi @Sam_H,

When you open Easyform, the form logo should automatically populate with a reference to the account logo. You can grab it from there.

Additionally, you can use third-party services such as postimg.cc to host the image. When sharing, select the “Direct link” option.

We’ve recently added support in Easyform for unlimited logo size, anywhere from 8px to the full width of the form.

You can check out our logo documentation page here.

Additionally, you can book a demo or support call with us here, or contact us by mail at easyappssupport@easyappshq.com.

posts.author: howardelias (Howard Elias)
posts.cookedHtml:
When it comes to logo size on forms, it’s crucial to balance visibility with layout. A logo should be large enough to be clearly recognizable, but not so big that it overwhelms the form’s content. Typically, a width of 150–300 pixels works well for digital forms, while printed forms may require a logo around 1–2 inches in width.

If you’re using a vintage logo illustration, consider that intricate details in classic designs might get lost if the logo is scaled down too much. Maintaining clarity and readability is key, so adjust the size proportionally and test it on a sample form before finalizing. Vintage logos often have ornamental elements, and sizing them correctly ensures your brand retains its authentic style while keeping the form professional and user-friendly.
